name: Umbrella Man
description: The "Umbrella Man" is a figure who appeared in the Zapruder film of the John F. Kennedy assassination in Dealey Plaza. The man is seen standing near the Stemmons Freeway overpass, opening and closing a black umbrella shortly before and during the shooting. Conspiracy theories propose that the Umbrella Man was a signalman involved in a larger plot to assassinate Kennedy. Proponents suggest the umbrella was a prearranged signal indicating the timing of the assassination, perhaps to other shooters or to confirm Kennedy's presence. Some theories link the umbrella to protests against Kennedy's father, Joseph P. Kennedy Sr.'s appeasement policies towards Nazi Germany before World War II, referencing British Prime Minister Neville Chamberlain's umbrella. The umbrella could have symbolized Kennedy Sr's appeasement, and opening it on a sunny day could be interpreted as a symbolic act of disapproval. However, the House Select Committee on Assassinations (HSCA) identified the man as Louie Steven Witt, who testified that he was protesting Kennedy's appeasement of Nazi Germany, a reference to JFK's father's policies. Witt brought the umbrella as a symbol of Chamberlain and his policies, and admitted he was unaware of the chaos he caused. Mainstream historians and researchers largely dismiss the Umbrella Man theory as lacking credible evidence, attributing it to misinterpretation of the Zapruder film and the human tendency to find patterns in random events. No credible evidence links the Umbrella Man to any assassination plot, and Witt's explanation is widely accepted.
subtitle: Dealey Plaza assassination conspiracy figure

claims.claim: The Umbrella Man was a signalman for assassins.
claims.evidence: His unusual behavior of opening and closing an umbrella on a sunny day.
claims.criticism: The man, Louie Steven Witt, identified himself and explained his actions as a protest against JFK's father's appeasement policies. No evidence links him to any assassination plot.

claims.claim: The umbrella symbolized opposition to Kennedy's policies.
claims.evidence: The umbrella's association with Neville Chamberlain and appeasement of Nazi Germany.
claims.criticism: Witt's testimony supports this interpretation, but it doesn't imply involvement in the assassination itself. It's a symbolic protest, not a signal.

claims.claim: The Umbrella Man was part of a larger conspiracy to kill JFK.
claims.evidence: The perceived coordination needed to time the assassination with his umbrella movements.
claims.criticism: There is no credible evidence to suggest any coordination. Witt's actions appear to be independent and unrelated to the shooting itself.

keyFigures.name: Louie Steven Witt
keyFigures.role: Identified as the Umbrella Man, explained his motives

keyFigures.name: Josiah Thompson
keyFigures.role: Early researcher who popularized the Umbrella Man theory

timeline.event: JFK assassination and appearance of the Umbrella Man
Louie Steven Witt identifies himself to the HSCA

sources.author: House Select Committee on Assassinations
sources.work: Report of the House Select Committee on Assassinations
